literal: with-upādhi, conditioned by upādhi; sa- (with) + upādhika
invariant_definition: Conditioned by *upādhi* — the Advaita technical category for that which is real only relative to a limiting condition. The *jīva* is *sopādhika brahman* (Brahman conditioned by *antaḥkaraṇa*-upādhi); *Īśvara* is *sopādhika brahman* (Brahman conditioned by *māyā*-upādhi). Contrasted with *nirupādhika brahman* — the unconditioned ultimate. Non-Advaita schools do not use the *sopādhika*-*nirupādhika* distinction technically because they reject the underlying *upādhi*-doctrine.

per_school.definition: *Sopādhika* names the *vyāvahārika* status of *jīva*, *Īśvara*, and the world: real-relative-to-upādhi, sublatable on removal of *upādhi*.
